note: Georgia has NOT fully expanded Medicaid. The Georgia Pathways program offers limited Medicaid to adults 19-64 at up to 100% FPL with work/community engagement requirements. Most working-age adults without children do not qualify for full Medicaid.

programs.name: SNAP (Georgia)
programs.description: Georgia administers federal SNAP. Apply through the Gateway portal.

programs.name: Georgia Medicaid / Pathways
programs.full_name: Georgia Medicaid and Pathways to Coverage
programs.description: Georgia Medicaid covers pregnant women (up to 220% FPL), children (up to 247% FPL), and people with disabilities/elderly. The Pathways program covers adults 19-64 at up to 100% FPL who meet work/community engagement requirements (80 hrs/month).

monthly_benefit_range.note: No premium for most enrollees. Georgia has NOT fully expanded Medicaid.

income.note: Pathways: adults 19-64 up to 100% FPL with 80 hrs/month work or community engagement. Georgia has NOT fully expanded Medicaid.

eligibility_rules.work_requirements: Pathways program requires 80 hours/month of work, job training, education, or volunteering

required_documents: Proof of qualifying work/community engagement (for Pathways)

programs.name: TANF (Georgia)
programs.full_name: Temporary Assistance for Needy Families — Georgia
programs.description: Georgia's TANF program provides modest cash to families with children. A family of 3 can receive up to approximately $280/month.

income.note: Very low income limits; work requirements apply

eligibility_rules.time_limit: 48-month state lifetime limit (stricter than federal 60 months)
